### get_graph_hash

>  get_graph_hash (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph)

*Returns a hash of the graph*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### create_iter_graph

>  create_iter_graph (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph,
>                         cycle:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph,
>                         anchor:str|int)

*Creates a graph for iterating over a cycle containig the cycle and the
ingress nodes*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/DeanLight/spannerflow/blob/main/spannerflow/graph_utils.py#L132"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### find_egress_node

>  find_egress_node (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph, cycle)

*returns the node that has an edge from the cycle to a node that is not
part of the cycle*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/DeanLight/spannerflow/blob/main/spannerflow/graph_utils.py#L120"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### find_ingress_nodes

>  find_ingress_nodes (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph, cycle)

*returns all nodes that have an edge to the cycle that is not part of
the cycle*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/DeanLight/spannerflow/blob/main/spannerflow/graph_utils.py#L110"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### traverse_cycle

>  traverse_cycle (cycle:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph, anchor:str|int)

*create a list of nodes for traversing the cylce (a direct edge must
exist between adjucent nodes in the list) The anchor node must be the
last node in the list.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/DeanLight/spannerflow/blob/main/spannerflow/graph_utils.py#L105"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### get_minus_cols

>  get_minus_cols (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph, node1:str|int,
>                      common_cols)

*Returns the columns that are in node1 but not in the common_cols*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/DeanLight/spannerflow/blob/main/spannerflow/graph_utils.py#L100"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### get_common_cols

>  get_common_cols (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph, node1:int|str,
>                       node2:int|str)

*Returns the common columns between two nodes*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/DeanLight/spannerflow/blob/main/spannerflow/graph_utils.py#L92"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

>  get_node_schema (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph, node:str|int)

*Returns the schema of a node in the graph - string representation of
the columns*

>  reduced_graph (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph)

*Returned a reduced graph with the cycle nodes removed but the anchor
node*

>  find_anchor_of_cycle (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph,
>                            cycle:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph)

>  get_cycles (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph)

*Returns a dictionary of cycles in the graph, with the anchor node as
the key*

### change_node_key

>  change_node_key (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph, old_key:str|int,
>                       new_key:str|int)

*Changes the key of a node in a graph - used to change anchor nodes
names.*

### find_output

>  find_output (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph)

*Returns the output node of the graph, i.e. the node with no outgoing
edges*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/DeanLight/spannerflow/blob/main/spannerflow/graph_utils.py#L14"

### find_sources

>  find_sources (graph:networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph)

*Returns the sources of the graph, i.e. the nodes with no incoming
edges*
